task_completion.description: The CT Cranium DICOM series is successfully loaded into InVesalius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface displays the medical images, showing axial, coronal, or sagittal views of a human skull/cranium.

task_completion.description: The angular measurement tool is activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The angle measurement tool (typically an icon resembling an angle or protractor) is highlighted or selected in the InVesalius toolbar or menu.

task_completion.description: A three-point angular measurement is performed on the skull anatomy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three distinct points connected by two lines forming an angle are visible, overlaid on the skull anatomy in one of the 2D slice views or the 3D surface view.

task_completion.description: The measured angle is within the anatomically valid range of 30 to 170 degrees.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numerical angle value is displayed on the screen next to the measurement lines, and this value is between 30.0 and 170.0 degrees.

task_completion.description: The InVesalius project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project As' dialog box shows the filename 'cranial_angle.inv3' in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory, OR the InVesalius window title updates to 'cranial_angle.inv3', OR a file explorer/terminal shows the file exists in the correct location.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the InVesalius application window (clicking menus, using tools), rather than just running scripts or terminal commands.

integrity.description: The measurement is genuinely performed on the medical image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement lines and text are rendered by the InVesalius software over the CT scan, not drawn using an external image editor or overlaid on a blank screen.

integrity.description: The project file was saved via the application, not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action should be visible via the InVesalius UI (Save dialog or window title change). The file should not be created using terminal commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/cranial_angle.inv3'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct output path for the saved project is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/cranial_angle.inv3'. The measured angle must be between 30.0 and 170.0 degrees to be considered valid. The DICOM series used is 'ct_cranium/0051'.
